data.title: Research by Barna Group and Gloo reveals AI's increasing influence in Americans' spiritual lives at National Religious Broadcasters Convention. New data will focus on Faith and AI trends with monthly releases.
data.description: At the National Religious Broadcasters International Christian Media Convention in Nashville, Tennessee, Barna Group and Gloo (Nasdaq: GLOO) unveiled new research from the State of the Church project regarding Americans' and Christians' utilization of AI. The joint initiative will notably emphasize Faith and AI trends this year, with monthly data releases planned for ongoing insights. This research will shed light on how AI is integrated into the lives of individuals, with regular updates expected in the coming months.

data.title: Marimekko Corp bought back shares on 19 Feb 2026.
data.description: Recently, it has been reported that the unemployment rate has risen to 6.2%, marking an increase of 0.3% from the previous month. The number of job losses reached 140,000, with the majority of the losses occurring in the retail and hospitality sectors. This rise in unemployment is attributed to ongoing economic challenges and restrictions related to the global pandemic. The government has announced plans to provide additional support for businesses and workers affected by the current situation. Moreover, there is a growing concern among experts about the long-term impact of this trend on the overall economy.

data.title: GTT 2025 results: Revenue up 25%, EBITDA up 40%. Vessel orders rising. Dividend at record €8.94 per share.
data.description: In the fiscal year 2025, there was significant progress in financial performance with a remarkable 25% increase in revenues and a substantial 40% growth in EBITDA for the third consecutive year. The company also experienced a notable surge in vessel orders starting from the fourth quarter of 2025. Additionally, a record-high dividend of €8.94 per share was declared for the 2025 period.

data.title: Uniglobe Travel Center wins ASTA Host Agency of the Year for 4th time. Award presented during ASTA Globes event in Calif.
data.description: Uniglobe Travel Center, based in Newport Beach, California, has earned the prestigious title of Host Agency of the Year from the American Society of Travel Advisors (ASTA). This notable recognition marks the fourth occasion that the company has been bestowed with this distinction. The award was conferred during the ASTA Globes event and was gratefully accepted by Uniglobe Travel Center.

data.title: ZenaTech's ZenaDrone subsidiary applies for Green UAS program with ZenaDrone 1000 to boost US defense contracts.
data.description: ZenaTech, Inc., a tech company based in Vancouver, disclosed key information on Feb. 19, 2026. The company is listed on NASDAQ with the symbol ZENA, on FSE as 49Q, and on BMV as ZENA. Specializing in AI, Drones as a Service, enterprise SaaS, and Quantum Computing solutions, ZenaTech shared important developments.

data.title: Financial Finesse appoints new VP of Financial Coaching in response to increased demand for guidance, emphasizing human-led AI-enhanced approach.
data.description: In an effort to address the increasing financial unease and growing need for consistent support, the company is strengthening its dedication to a people-focused coaching approach with 26 years of established success. Situated in EL SEGUNDO, Calif., on Feb. 19, 2026, the organization aims to tackle record levels of financial stress across the country.

data.title: Inventor from Mountlake Terrace, Wash., creates TEFF CHIPS, a new gluten-free snack without artificial additives.
data.description: In Pittsburgh on February 19, 2026, an inventor from Mountlake Terrace, Washington shared that they aimed to develop a tasty gluten-free snack chip without artificial additives or preservatives. This led to the creation of TEFF CHIPS. The innovative formula guarantees a satisfying crunch and is free from gluten, providing a flavorful and natural snacking option.

data.title: eBay's Q4 results beat expectations, analysts raise price target: JPMorgan $87, Goldman Sachs $88. eBay acquires Depop.
data.description: EBay Inc's shares (NASDAQ: EBAY) surged during early trading following the company's positive fourth-quarter results. Analyst insights reveal key details: JPMorgan's Doug Anmuth maintained a Neutral rating, with a price target increase from $84 to $87. Meanwhile, Goldman Sachs analyst Eric Sheridan also retained a Neutral rating and raised the price target from $77 to $88. JPMorgan noted eBay's strong quarterly performance, with GMV, revenue, and margins exceeding expectations. The company's 2026 GMV guidance in line with 2025, surpassing estimates as eBay demonstrates strong execution of strategic plans amid international challenges. In other news, eBay announced its acquisition of Depop from Etsy Inc. For more details, refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Etsy reported Q4 2025 results, with $3.6B GMS, $882M revenue, $222M adjusted EBITDA. Marketplace GMS up 0.1%, with positive growth in US buyer GMS for the first time in 4 years. Depop was sold to eBay for $1.2B. CEO Kruthi Patel Goyal discussed strategies to drive growth and enhance customer experience. CFO Lanny Baker highlighted positive momentum in marketplace results and Etsy's differentiation strategies.
data.description: Etsy reported its fourth-quarter and full-year financial results, with the company's stock seeing gains. The newly appointed CEO, Kruthi Patel Goyal, discussed strategic priorities and positive performance improvements in the core marketplace. The company signed an agreement to sell Depop to eBay for $1.2 billion. In the fourth quarter, Etsy's Marketplace GMS was $3.6 billion, up 2.4% year over year. Consolidated revenue reached $882 million, up 6.6% excluding Reverb. Adjusted EBITDA was $222 million, with a margin of 25.2%. Etsy Marketplace GMS was slightly above 30% in Q4, while US buyer GMS grew for the first time in four years. Mobile app downloads grew 4% year over year, contributing 46% to total GMS. Depop's GMS reached $300 million in Q4, with growth of nearly 38% year over year. The transaction to sell Depop will allow Etsy to focus on growing its core marketplace.

data.title: Curi Advisory introduces Revenue Cycle Solutions, enhancing support with Chad Peters as Managing Director.
data.description: In an effort to enhance its recurring support services, Curi Advisory has introduced a new offering, appointing Chad Peters as the Managing Director of Revenue Cycle Solutions. This strategic move is aimed at bolstering Curi's capabilities within the healthcare sector. The announcement was made in Raleigh, N.C., on Feb. 19, 2026. This reinforces Curi Advisory's commitment to providing embedded support to clients, with a focus on optimizing performance. Peters' role will be crucial in overseeing revenue cycle solutions for the company, furthering its impact in the industry.

data.title: Coface closes 2025 with €222.0m net income, 197% solvency, €1.25 dividend per share; turnover at €1,847m, up 1.3%.
data.description: Coface had a successful year in 2025, with a net income of €222.0m and a solvency ratio of 197%. The company reported a turnover of €1,847m, with insurance revenue up 0.6% and non-insurance activities growing by 7.8%. The client retention rate was high at 92.9%, while pricing remained negative at -1.6%. The net combined ratio was at 73.1%, slightly above mid-cycle targets. The company's earnings per share reached €1.49, with a proposed dividend of €1.25 per share, representing an 84% payout ratio. CEO Xavier Durand highlighted the challenges faced in the global market but praised the company's strategic execution.

data.title: Trump claimed a 78% reduction in trade deficit, but data shows a $901.5 billion deficit in 2025, almost same as 2024.
data.description: In 2025, America's trade deficit was a key focus, with President Donald Trump implementing tariffs to reduce it. Trump claimed in a tweet that the deficit had decreased by 78% and would turn positive for the first time in decades. However, official data showed a different picture - in December, the trade deficit was $70.3 billion, higher than economist predictions. The total deficit for the year was $901.5 billion, similar to the previous year. Despite Trump's tariffs, the trade balance remained in the negative. This information challenges Trump's claim of a significant reduction in the trade deficit.

data.title: Esports industry to hit $9.2B by 2033 with 21.2% CAGR, driven by online games, mobile gaming, and tech advancements.
data.description: The esports industry is experiencing significant expansion driven by the increased interest in online video games, higher adoption of mobile gaming, and advancements in technology. A recent report by Allied Market Research revealed that the esports market is poised for substantial growth. This growth is attributed to various factors such as the growing popularity of online video games and the increasing penetration of mobile gaming. The report, titled "Esports," highlights the favorable market conditions that are contributing to this trend. The study, based on industry analysis, projects promising opportunities in the esports sector, with expected revenue reaching impressive figures. The city of Portland, Oregon, was the site of the report's publication on February 19, 2026, as per PRNewswire. These developments underscore the flourishing nature of the esports market, indicating a bright future ahead.
Sanoma Corp buys back shares on 19/02/2026.

data.title: Six Flags revenue beats expectations despite wider loss. Attendance down 13%, but spending up 8%, reaching $650 million.
data.description: Six Flags Entertainment Corporation's (NYSE: FUN) stock saw an increase following the release of its financial results. The company reported a fourth-quarter adjusted loss of 91 cents per share, much wider than the anticipated loss of 22 cents per share. Despite a decrease in attendance by 13%, totaling 9.3 million guests, the theme park operator experienced a rise in per capita spending. Quarterly sales were reported at $650.089 million, surpassing the predicted $603.053 million. On a per-operating-day basis, attendance decreased by 2% year over year. The per capita spending rose by 8% to $66.41, with admissions contributing $35.32 and in-park purchases at $31.10. The increase in admissions spending was attributed to pricing strategies, promotions, and shifts in attendance mix. In-park spending growth was driven by...
